question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user who gains write access to the Redis pub/sub channel (e.g., via an exposed Redis port with no auth) publish a crafted TopicMessage with a forged runningHash to the channel consumed by subscribe(), causing all downstream gRPC subscribers to receive a falsified Hashgraph running hash without any integrity check?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with an arbitrary sequenceNumber into the Redis channel, bypassing any monotonicity or gap-detection check in subscribe(), causing clients to observe a falsified sequence number that misrepresents the true Hashgraph message order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user publish a TopicMessage with a manipulated consensusTimestamp to the Redis channel, and since subscribe() applies no timestamp validation against the actual consensus ledger, cause gRPC clients to receive a message with a falsified consensus time that rewrites perceived Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user predict the Redis channel name produced by getTopic() (format 'topic.%d') for any known topicId, and use that predictable channel name to inject forged TopicMessage objects that tamper with the perceived history of that topic for all active subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of cryptographic signature verification in subscribe()'s messageSerializer deserialization path to inject a TopicMessage with a completely fabricated message payload, causing clients to believe a false message was submitted to the Hashgraph topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a runningHashVersion set to an invalid or downgraded value into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a message that signals a weaker hash algorithm was used, misleading clients about the integrity guarantees of the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user publish a TopicMessage with a spoofed topicId (different from the channel's actual topic) into the Redis channel, and since subscribe() does not re-validate the deserialized message's topicId against the subscribed channel name, cause cross-topic message injection that corrupts the perceived history of a target topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Flux produced by getSharedListener() via the topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap to ensure that a single injected forged TopicMessage is broadcast to all concurrent subscribers of that topic, amplifying the impact of a single Redis injection into a mass history-tampering event?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a sequenceNumber lower than the last seen sequence number into the Redis channel, and since subscribe() performs no sequence number rollback detection, cause clients to observe a replayed or rewound Hashgraph sequence that misrepresents the true message history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a null or zeroed runningHash byte array into the Redis channel, and since subscribe() passes the deserialized message directly to subscribers without hash chain validation, cause clients to accept a message that breaks the SHA-384 running hash chain of the Hashgraph topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a crafted chunkNum and chunkTotal into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a falsified chunked message assembly that misrepresents the original large message submitted to the Hashgraph topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a spoofed payerAccountId into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a message that falsely attributes a Hashgraph topic submission to a different account, tampering with the perceived transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a manipulated initialTransactionId into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to propagate a message that falsely links the topic submission to a different originating transaction, corrupting the Hashgraph transaction chain visible to clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Jackson @JsonTypeInfo polymorphic deserialization used by messageSerializer to inject a specially crafted Redis payload that deserializes as a malicious TopicMessage subtype, causing subscribe() to propagate a tampered object that misrepresents Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the Redis channel with a high volume of forged TopicMessage objects with sequential but fabricated sequenceNumbers, causing subscribe() to deliver a completely synthetic Hashgraph message history to all subscribers before any legitimate messages arrive?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a consensusTimestamp set far in the past into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a backdated message that makes clients believe a Hashgraph consensus event occurred at a falsified earlier time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a consensusTimestamp set far in the future into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a future-dated message that disrupts the chronological ordering of Hashgraph history perceived by clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a message byte array containing arbitrary content into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a falsified message body to clients who trust the mirror node as an authoritative source of Hashgraph topic history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with sequenceNumber=1 and a fabricated runningHash into the Redis channel for a topic that already has a long history, causing subscribe() to deliver a message that appears to reset the Hashgraph topic's sequence to the beginning, misleading clients about the topic's true state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition in getSharedListener()'s computeIfAbsent on the topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap by sending concurrent subscription requests, causing two separate subscribe() Flux instances to be created for the same topic, resulting in duplicate or out-of-order message delivery that corrupts the perceived Hashgraph sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated subscribe/unsubscribe cycles by connecting and disconnecting, causing unsubscribe() to remove the topic from topicMessages and forcing a new subscribe() call that re-reads from the Redis channel from the current position, silently dropping historical messages and creating gaps in the Hashgraph history delivered to clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)) in subscribe() by repeatedly causing Redis connection errors (e.g., via connection flooding), forcing the listener to reconnect and miss messages published during the retry window, creating artificial gaps in the Hashgraph history stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the share() operator in subscribe() such that if one subscriber's backpressure causes a BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR in the parent SharedTopicListener.listen(), the shared Flux terminates for all subscribers, causing all clients to lose their live Hashgraph history stream simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a duplicate consensusTimestamp (matching an already-delivered message) into the Redis channel, causing subscribe() to deliver a duplicate that, when processed by downstream services, creates an inconsistent view of the Hashgraph topic's message history?
